mainEntity.name: I had a bad experience with an SEO agency—how can I approach featured snippets for voice search now without getting burned again?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you’ve had a bad experience with an SEO vendor, treat featured-snippet optimization as a transparent, measurable program rather than a black box.

Most of the work can be documented as specific content and schema changes you can review yourself.

**Key factors:**
- Require clear before/after examples of Q&A structures on target pages
- Insist on access to Search Console and analytics to see real results
- Use small pilots (5–10 pages) to prove value before scaling
- Avoid vague promises; focus on specific queries and snippet formats

Whether you do this in-house or with a new partner, define success upfront: which questions you want to own, how answers will be written, and what metrics you’ll watch. Review copy as if you’re the end user.

A structured, evidence-based approach prevents the “trust me” problems that caused issues before.
acceptedAnswer.description: Treat featured snippet work as a transparent, testable program: start with a small set of pages, review the exact Q&A and schema changes, and monitor Search Console and analytics yourself. Focus on explicit target queries and measurable snippet formats, not vague promises or black-box tactics.
